name: HNMS O 15 (N 15 / P 15)
events: The history of HrMs O 15 as compiled on this page is extracted from the patrol reports and logbooks of this submarine found at the British archives at Kew, London and the Dutch archives at The Hague, Netherlands.
This page was last updated in February 2019.

The following photographs are taken by Charles James Sadler, RCNVR. First Class Stoker, Official number V-4963, serving in the Canadian destroyer HMCS Columbia. They show HrMs O-15 while serving as target ship at Halifax, Canada in 1941 or early 1942.

2 Oct 1939 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) and HrMs O 20 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Bussemaker, RNN) departed Den Helder, Netherlands for the Dutch West Indies. They were escorted by the gunboat HrMs Van Kinsbergen (Cdr. J.L.K. Hoeke, RNN).
For the daily positions of HrMs O 15 during this passage see the map below.

14 Oct 1939 HrMs O 20 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Bussemaker, RNN) and HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) and their escort HrMs Van Kinsbergen (Cdr. J.L.K. Hoeke, RNN) arrived at Ponta Delgada, Azores.

18 Oct 1939 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) and HrMs O 20 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Bussemaker, RNN) and their escort HrMs Van Kinsbergen (Cdr. J.L.K. Hoeke, RNN) departed Ponta Delgada, Azores to continue their passage to the Dutch West Indies (Curacao). (1)

31 Oct 1939 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) and HrMs O 20 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Bussemaker, RNN) and their escort HrMs Van Kinsbergen (Cdr. J.L.K. Hoeke, RNN) arrive at Willemstad, Curacao, Dutch West Indies. (1)
13 Nov 1939 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) was docked at Willemstad, Curacao, Dutch West Indies. (1)
14 Nov 1939 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) was undocked. (1)
19 Nov 1939 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) departed Willemstad, Curacao for Aruba. (1)
20 Nov 1939 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) arrived at Oranjestad, Aruba, Dutch West Indies. (1)
27 Nov 1939 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Oranjestad, Aruba, to Willemstad, Curacao. (1)
18 Dec 1939 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Willemstad, Curacao to Oranjestad, Aruba. (1)
2 Jan 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Oranjestad, Aruba, to Willemstad, Curacao. (1)
11 Jan 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Willemstad, Curacao to Oranjestad, Aruba. (2)
22 Jan 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Oranjestad, Aruba, to Willemstad, Curacao. (2)
5 Feb 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Willemstad, Curacao to Oranjestad, Aruba. (2)
19 Feb 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Oranjestad, Aruba, to Willemstad, Curacao. (2)
28 Feb 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) was docked at Willemstad, Curacao, Dutch West Indies. (2)
2 Mar 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) was undocked. (2)
4 Mar 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Willemstad, Curacao to Oranjestad, Aruba. (2)
18 Mar 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Oranjestad, Aruba, to Willemstad, Curacao. (2)
8 Apr 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Willemstad, Curacao to Oranjestad, Aruba. (2)
18 Apr 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Oranjestad, Aruba, to Willemstad, Curacao. (3)
1 May 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Willemstad, Curacao to Oranjestad, Aruba. (3)
6 May 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Oranjestad, Aruba, to Willemstad, Curacao. (3)
10 May 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Willemstad, Curacao to Oranjestad, Aruba. (3)
12 May 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Oranjestad, Aruba, to Kralendijk, Bonaire. (3)
15 May 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) shifted from Kralendijk, Bonaire to Willemstad, Curacao.
During the coming period a self refit was conducted by the crew of O 15. (3)
27 Jun 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) was docked at Willemstad, Curacao, Dutch West Indies. (3)
28 Jun 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) was undocked. She continued her self refit at Willemstad. (3)

13 Jul 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) ended her refit at Curacao. She departed for Bermuda the same day. She made the passage together with the British sloop HMS Dundee (Capt.(Retd.) O.M.F. Stokes, RN).
O 15 was to proceed to Halifax, Canada. There she was to join a convoy for onward passage to the U.K. where she was to join the 9th Submarine Flotilla based at Dundee.
19 Jul 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) arrived at Hamilton, Bermuda.

During passage engine defects developed. It was intended to tow O 15 to the U.K. for repairs. This however was rejected and it was decided to stick to the original plan and sent her to Halifax. At Halifax repairs would be made before onward passage to the U.K. (3)
2 Aug 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) departed Bermuda for Halifax. (3)

6 Aug 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) arrived at Halifax. Repairs were now undertaken to the engines. This had to be done mostly be her own crew with the assistence of the Halifax naval dockyard. (3)
5 Oct 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) was docked at Halifax. (3)
31 Oct 1940 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) was undocked. (3)

13 Mar 1941 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) ended her refit at Halifax. Meanwhile it had been decided that O 15 would stay in Canada to aid in A/S training for the rapidly expanding Royal Canadian Navy.
No logs of O 15 are available for the period March 1941 / May 1942 therefore few details are known for this period. (3)
29 Jan 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) departed Halifax for Philadelphia, U.S.A. At the Navy yard there she is to be refitted. (4)
5 Feb 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) arrived at the Philadelphia Navy Yard and commenced a refit. (4)
25 May 1942 With her refit completed HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed the Philadelphia Navy Yard for the U.S. submarine base at New London, Connecticut.
27 May 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at New London, Connecticut, USA for a period of trials and training. (5)
12 Jun 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed New London for Halifax, Canada. After almost two year she is finally to proceed to the U.K.
15 Jun 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Halifax.
Before she could join a convoy to the U.K. the submarine that had replaced her on A/S training duties (HMS P 514 (Lt.Cdr. R.M.E. Pain, RN)) was lost in an accident.
As the training program of the RCN could not do without a training submarine it was decided that O 15 would be used for training again until she could be replaced by another submarine.

HMS P 553 (Lt. L.F.L. Hill, RNR) was commissioned at New London on 14 September 1942, she was to take over from HrMs O 15 which now was finally able to proceed to the U.K. Two years after it was originally intened to do so. (5)

26 Jun 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Halifax for Pictou. She was escorted by the Canadian corvettes HMCS Ville de Quebec (T/Lt.Cdr. D.G. Jeffrey, DSO, RCNR) and HMCS Port Arthur (T/Lt. E.T. Simmons, DSC, RCNVR). (6)
27 Jun 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Pictou. (6)
30 Aug 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Pictou for Halifax. (6)
31 Aug 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Halifax. (6)
15 Sep 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Halifax for St.Johns. She was escorted by the Canadian corvette HMCS Alberni (T/Lt. A.W. Ford, RCNR).
17 Sep 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at St.Johns. (7)
19 Sep 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed St.Johns for the U.K. (7)

28 Sep 1942 At 1850 hours, HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)), made rendez-vous South off Barrahead with the British ASW trawler HMS Preston North End (Lt. K.A. Vasey, MBE, RNR). Both ships set course for Scapa Flow through the Minches. (7)
30 Sep 1942 At 0630 hours, HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)), arrived at Scapa Flow.
At 1200 hours she departed for Dundee escorted by the British ASW trawler HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (7)
1 Oct 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Dundee. (7)
23 Oct 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Dundee for Lerwick. She was escorted by the trawler HMS River Lossie.
At 0800/24 HMS Leicester City (T/Lt. C.E. Irving, RNVR) took over the escort. (7)
24 Oct 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Lerwick. (7)

25 Oct 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Lerwick for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Norwegian coast and later to take up a patrol position in the Norwegian Sea to give cover for convoy operation to and from Russia.
For the daily positions of HrMs O 15 during this patrol see the map below.
16 Nov 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) ended her 1st war patrol at Lerwick. (7)
17 Nov 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Lerwick for Dundee. She was escorted by the British ASW trawler HMS Preston North End (Lt. K.A. Vasey, MBE, RNR). (7)
18 Nov 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Dundee. (7)
22 Nov 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) shifted from Dundee to Rosyth. She was escorted by HMS ML 443 (T/Lt. T.D.L. Platt, DSO, RNVR) (8)
27 Nov 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) was docked at Rosyth. (8)
4 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) was undocked. (8)
7 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) shifted from Rosyth to Dundee.
10 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) departed Dundee for Scapa Flow. She was escorted by the British ASW trawler HMS Preston North End (Lt. K.A. Vasey, MBE, RNR). (8)
11 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) arrived at Scapa Flow where she was to participate in A/S exercises. (8)
14 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Matchless (Lt.Cdr. J. Mowlam, RN) and HMS Bermuda (Capt. T.H. Back, RN). (8)
15 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Loyal (Lt.Cdr. H.E.F. Tweedie, DSC, RN) and Adrias. (8)

16 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Queenborough (Cdr. E.P. Hinton, DSO and Bar, MVO, RN) and HMS Relentless (Lt.Cdr. R.A. Fell, RN). (8)

17 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Queenborough (Cdr. E.P. Hinton, DSO and Bar, MVO, RN) and HMS Relentless (Lt.Cdr. R.A. Fell, RN). (8)

18 Dec 1942 In the afternoon, HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMCS Iroquois (Cdr. W.B.L. Holms, RCN). HMCS Iroquois was escorting HMS Ajax (Capt. J.J. Weld, MVO, RN) on which HrMs O 15 conducted practice attacks. (8)

19 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Relentless (Lt.Cdr. R.A. Fell, RN) and HMS Goathland (Lt.Cdr. E.N. Pumphrey, RN, DSO and Bar, DSC, RN) (8)
21 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Relentless (Lt.Cdr. R.A. Fell, RN) and HMCS Iroquois (Cdr. W.B.L. Holms, RCN). (8)

22 Dec 1942 In late morning and the afternoon, HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Relentless (Lt.Cdr. R.A. Fell, RN) and HMCS Iroquois (Cdr. W.B.L. Holms, RCN). (8)
23 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Relentless (Lt.Cdr. R.A. Fell, RN) and RHS Adrias. (8)
24 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Mackay (Capt. S.V. Jephson, RN) and ORP Piorun (Cdr. T. Gorazdowski). (8)

24 Dec 1942 In the afternoon, HMS Penelope (Capt. G.D. Belben, DSC, AM, RN), conducted an oiling at sea exercise at Scapa flow with the RFA tanker Blue Ranger. This exercise was followed by an A/S exercise with a submarine. [This was most likely HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN).] (9)
26 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMCS Iroquois (Cdr. W.B.L. Holms, RCN) and HMS Walpole (Lt. A.S. Pomeroy, DSC, RN).

[According to the log of HrMs O 15, HMCS Iroquois participated in these exercises. This is however impossible as according to her own log, HMCS Iroquois was in the floating dock at Scapa Flow undergoing repairs to her hull.] (8)
28 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Quadrant (Lt.Cdr. W.H. Farrington, RN) and HMS Raider (Lt.Cdr. K.W. Michell, RN). (8)
29 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Quadrant (Lt.Cdr. W.H. Farrington, RN) and HMS Worcester (Lt.Cdr. W.A. Juniper, RN). (8)
30 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Quadrant (Lt.Cdr. W.H. Farrington, RN). (8)
31 Dec 1942 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Musketeer (Cdr. E.N.V. Currey, DSC, RN) and ORP Orkan (Cdr. S. Hryniewiecki, ORP). (8)
1 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Quadrant (Lt.Cdr. W.H. Farrington, RN) and Miaoulis. (8)

2 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow, first with HMCS Iroquois (Cdr. W.B.L. Holms, RCN) and HMS Quadrant (Lt.Cdr. W.H. Farrington, RN) and later with HMS Vivacious (Lt.Cdr. R. Alexander, RN) and ORP Orkan (Kmdr.por. (Cdr.) S. Hryniewiecki). (8)

4 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Vivacious (Lt.Cdr. R. Alexander, RN), HMS Windsor (Lt.Cdr. D.H.F. Hetherington, RN) and Adrias. (8)
5 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Onslaught (Cdr. W.H. Selby, RN) and HMS Brecon (Lt.Cdr. T.D. Herrick, DSC and Bar, RN). (8)

6 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Quadrant (Lt.Cdr. W.H. Farrington, RN), HMCS Iroquois (Cdr. W.B.L. Holms, RCN) and RHS Miaoulis (?). (8)
7 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Forester (Lt.Cdr. J.A. Burnett, DSC, RN) and HMS Easton (Lt. C.W. Malins, DSC, RN). (8)
8 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Matchless (Lt.Cdr. J. Mowlam, RN) and ORP Piorun (Cdr. T. Gorazdowski). (8)

9 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Queenborough (Cdr. E.P. Hinton, DSO and Bar, MVO, RN), HMS Middleton (Lt. C.S. Battersby, RN), HMS Offa (Cdr. R.A. Ewing, DSC, RN) and HMS Brecon (Lt.Cdr. T.D. Herrick, DSC and Bar, RN). (8)

11 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Middleton (Lt. C.S. Battersby, RN), HMS Easton (Lt. C.W. Malins, DSC, RN) and HMS Brecon (Lt.Cdr. T.D. Herrick, DSC and Bar, RN). (8)
13 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Brecon (Lt.Cdr. T.D. Herrick, DSC and Bar, RN) and ORP Orkan (Cdr. S. Hryniewiecki, ORP). (8)

14 Jan 1943 In the morining, HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Brecon (Lt.Cdr. T.D. Herrick, DSC and Bar, RN) and HMCS Iroquois (Cdr. W.B.L. Holms, RCN). (8)
15 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Onslaught (Cdr. W.H. Selby, RN) and HMS Easton (Lt. C.W. Malins, DSC, RN). (8)

18 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at / off Scapa Flow with HMS Queenborough (Cdr. E.P. Hinton, DSO and Bar, MVO, RN) and HMS Brecon (Lt.Cdr. T.D. Herrick, DSC and Bar, RN). (8)
19 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises with aircraft and destroyers (not specified in O 15's log). (8)
20 Jan 1943 In the morning, HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMCS Iroquois (Cdr. W.B.L. Holms, RCN) and HMS Easton (Lt. C.W. Malins, DSC, RN).
In the afternoon, HMCS Iroquois and HMS Easton conducted towing exercises at Scapa Flow during which the former took the latter in tow. (10)
21 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) departed Scapa Flow for Lerwick. She was escorted by the British ASW trawler HMS Lord Lloyd (T/Lt. M.W. Bond, RNVR). (8)
22 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) arrived at Lerwick. (8)
23 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) departed Lerwick for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Kristiansund, Norway.
26 Jan 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) is ordered to leave her patrol area and take up a position off Northern Norway, off the Altafjord. (7)
7 Feb 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) ended her 2nd war patrol at Lerwick. (7)

9 Feb 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) departed Lerwick for Dundee. She made the passage together with HMS P 224 (Lt. J.R. Drummond, DSC, RN), FFS Junon (Cdr. J. Querville, RN) and ORP Sokol (Lt.Cdr. J. Koziolkowski). They were escorted by HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (8)
10 Feb 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) arrived at Dundee. (8)
24 Feb 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) shifted from Dundee to Rosyth. She was escorted by HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (8)
26 Feb 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) was docked at Rosyth. (8)

3 Mar 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) was undocked. She immediately departed Rosyth for Dundee where she arrived later the same day. During the passage she was escorted by the British Motor Launch HMS ML 454 (T/Lt. W.M. Russell, RNVR).
At Dundee she commenced a refit which included the fitting of a whole lot of new equipment including radar, Asdic and an echo sounder just to name a few. (8)
11 Jun 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) commenced trials off Dundee. (8)
17 Jun 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) departed Dundee for Lerwick. She was escorted by HMS Loch Monteith (T/Lt. K.W. Richardson, RNR). (8)
18 Jun 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) arrived at Lerwick. (8)

19 Jun 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) departed Lerwick for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to perform a anti-uboat patrol in the Norwegian Sea first in position FF (approximately 63°30'N, 01°30'E) and later in position DD (approximately 64°30 N, 01°30'W).
3 Jul 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) ended her 3rd war patrol at Lerwick. (7)
4 Jul 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) departed Lerwick for Dundee. She was escorted by the MS trawler HMS Coll (T/Lt. E.V. Nash, RNR). (11)
5 Jul 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) arrived at Dundee. (11)
19 Aug 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) departed Dundee for Scapa Flow. She was escorted by the British Motor Launch HMS ML 254 (T/Lt. W.M. Russell, RNVR). (11)

For the daily and attack positions of HrMs O 15 during this patrol see the map below.

26 Dec 1943 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) fires three torpedoes against a German convoy in the Skudenes Fjord off the Southern entrance of Karmsundet in position 59°07'N, 05°24'E. The Norwegian merchant vessel Roald Jarl (1404 GRT, built 1913, offsite link) reports spotting two torpedo wakes, one of which passed underneath her.
(All times are zone -1) 1155 hours - Sighted two or more merchant ships proceeding South in Karmsundet.

1157 hours - It was now seen that a convoy of five or more ships was steaming South in Karmsundet on a course of 173°. Started attack. The escort was an armed trawler was leading the convoy. Range was 2000 yards. O 15 was however not in a favourable attack position and the convoy was likely to alter course shortly. O 15 went to 60 feet and manouvered to obtain a better attack position.
1210 hours - Returned to periscope depth. Saw that the convoy was made up of two rows of three ships each. It also had made the anticipated change of course. Continued attack.
1214 hours - Transmissions were now heard and it appeared the escort was in contact. These transmissions were heard during the remainder of the attack.

1225 hours - Fired three torpedoes. These were aimed one each at the leading ships of both columns and the rear ship of the second column. Three explosions were heard shortly afterwards. Asdic transmissions from the escort were now clearly audible inside the submarine and shortly afterwards the trawler passed overhead but did not drop any depth charges.

1250 hours - The stern torpedo tube was fired at the escort but no hit was obtained as the tube was fired prematurely due to a mechanical defect. O 15 meanwhile retired to seaward but no counter attack followed. No further observations could be made due to the bad visibility.

[ The escort seen by O 15 was the German armed trawler UJ 1712 (527 tons, built 1942). The ships in the convoy were the above mentioned Roald Jarl and most likely the Dora Oldendorf (2671 GRT, built 1896), Eisberg (492 GRT, built 1940), Thielbek (2815 GRT, built 1940) and Vesta (1620 GRT, built 1921). It is possible German minesweeper M 421 was also part of the escort. ] (7)

13 Oct 1944 While en-route from Campbeltown to the exercise area HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) is rammed by HMS Bretwalda (Skr. J.B. Henderson, RNR). The next morning, during a dive, minor damage to the Starboard propeller and the pressure hull is discovered. Arrangements were made for repairs.
After being rammed O 15 continued to the exercise area and conducted A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS St. Modwen (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Lycett, DSC, RNR) and HMS Valena (T/Lt. H. Trefusis, RNVR). (17)

18 Nov 1944 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) departed Rothesay for Dundee. She made the passage together with HrMs Zeehond (Lt.Cdr. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN). The submarines were escorted by HMS Castlenau (T/Skr. G. Wood, RNR). (17)
19 Nov 1944 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN), HrMs Zeehond (Lt.Cdr. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) and their escort HMS Castlenau (T/Skr. G. Wood, RNR) arrive at Tobermory. (17)
20 Nov 1944 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN), HrMs Zeehond (Lt.Cdr. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) and their escort HMS Castlenau (T/Skr. G. Wood, RNR) shifted from Tobemory to Loch Alsh. (17)
21 Nov 1944 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN), HrMs Zeehond (Lt.Cdr. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) and their escort HMS Castlenau (T/Skr. G. Wood, RNR) shifted from Loch Alsh to Stornoway. (17)
23 Nov 1944 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN), HrMs Zeehond (Lt.Cdr. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) and their escort HMS Castlenau (T/Skr. G. Wood, RNR) shifted from Stornoway to Loch Eriboll. (17)

25 Nov 1944 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN), HrMs Zeehond (Lt.Cdr. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) and their escort HMS Castlenau (T/Skr. G. Wood, RNR) arrived at Dundee. (17)
26 Nov 1944 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) commenced a refit at Dundee. The refit was done for the most part by her own crew. (17)
8 Jan 1945 With her (self-)refit completed HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) is now used for the training of Dutch submarine crews. (17)
20 Feb 1945 HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) and HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) both conducted exercises of Dundee. They made pratice attacks on each other. (17)
21 Feb 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) conducted a practive attack on HrMs Zeehond (Lt. S.H. de Boer, RNN). (17)
22 Feb 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) and HrMs Zeehond (Lt. S.H. de Boer, RNN) both conducted exercises of Dundee. They made pratice attacks on each other. (17)
28 Feb 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) and HrMs Zeehond (Lt. S.H. de Boer, RNN) both conducted exercises of Dundee. They made pratice attacks on each other. (17)

7 Mar 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) and HrMs Dolfijn (Lt.Cdr. J.B.M.J. Maas, RNN) both conducted exercises of Dundee. O 15 made one practice attack on Dolfijn. (17)

9 Mar 1945 HrMs Dolfijn (Lt.Cdr. J.B.M.J. Maas, RNN) conducted exercises off Dundee together with HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) and HMS Commodator (T/Skr. J.B. Davidson, RNR). (18)

28 Mar 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) and HrMs Zeehond (Lt. S.H. de Boer, RNN) both conducted exercises of Dundee. They made pratice attacks on each other. (17)
30 Apr 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) was docked at Dundee. (17)
23 May 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) was undocked. (17)
27 Jun 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) departed Dundee for Rotterdam.
30 Jun 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) arrived at Rotterdam.
23 Jul 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) departed Rotterdam for Dundee.
25 Jul 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) arrived at Dundee.
12 Sep 1945 HrMs O 15 (Lt.Cdr. R.W. van Lynden, RNN) was decommissioned at Dundee.
10 Dec 1945 The decommissioned O 15 departed Dundee for Rotterdam where she arrived on 12 December 1945. At Rotterdam she was used as accomodation vessel for a while.
